channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Capital One has a new tool to help keep track of all your subscriptions
item.description: Capital One launched on Wednesday a free, integrated subscription management tool, allowing customers to track, block, and cancel recurring payments from over 10,000 merchants directly through its mobile app.
item.dc:creator: Janya Sundar, Fast Company

item.title: How Libya and Tuvalu are cashing in on their top-level domain names
item.description: Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it’s not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Ghosting: When Is It Okay to Ignore Someone Online?
item.description: Understanding the nuances of ghosting and setting boundaries in online relationships
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: How Wikipedia is staying relevant in the AI era
item.description: Despite the rise of such AI models as ChatGPT and Google Gemini, “old-school” Wikipedia remains one of the most visited sites around the world—with 15 billion visitors every month.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: IBM and AWS: An alternative cloud path for VMWare workloads
item.description: In the post-Broadcom acquisition period, new subscription models and partner relationship changes are prompting VMware enterprises to reevaluate their IT estates. The goal: Determining the best path towards modernization and cloud migration.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Damac Group expands into Spain with 400 million euros data center investment in Madrid
item.description: The data center market in Spain continues to heat up with the latest major development from Dubai-based Damac Group. The company has announced its entry into the Spanish market with the acquisition of land in Madrid, where it plans to build a state-of-the-art data center.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: WasmGC and the future of front-end Java development
item.description: JavaScript is the clear choice for front-end development today, but there is a longstanding push to be able to use Java on the front end. Among other things, Java brings a vast ecosystem of libraries, frameworks, and application code, which can be exposed via its APIs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cómo abordar la deuda técnica antes de que cause estragos al negocio
item.description: En un escenario corporativo en el que la tecnología ha pasado a ostentar un papel protagonista al situarse cada vez más cerca del negocio, gestionar la deuda técnica derivada de la inversión en TI puede suponer un verdadero quebradero de cabeza para los directores de Sistemas de la Información (CIO,
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Microsoft became a Big Tech choirboy
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Department of Justice have aggressively targeted Big Tech, suing Meta, Google, Amazon, and Apple for antitrust violations. And it’s not doing so in a small way — it’s filed multiple law suits against Google, for example.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google signs deal to use small modular nuclear reactors to power AI data centers
item.description: Google has signed a deal with Kairos Power to build a fleet of small modular nuclear reactors to power its data centers, the tech giant announced Monday. The small modular reactors, a type of next-generation nuclear reactor that has yet to be built in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Voter beware: Text scam targets you, no matter where you live
item.description: Reports warn of a scam text falsely claiming that recipients aren’t registered to vote. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals what you need to know and five tips to avoid such schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: Hong Kong’s oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's 'Cybercab' as stock sinks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tesla unveiled its long-awaited robotaxi at a Hollywood studio Thursday night, though fans of the electric vehicle maker will have to wait until at least 2026 before they are available. CEO Elon Musk pulled up to a stage at the Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER and ALEX VEIGA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senators press Commerce Department to boost transparency about CHIPS Act contracts
item.description: A group of senators are urging the Commerce Department to be more transparent regarding the terms of agreements made under the CHIPS and Science Act, arguing the agency must ensure the U.S. semiconductor industry is “safe and sustainable.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people
item.description: "We could view cybersecurity as an entry level problem, but it is probably more of an intermediate and advanced role problem," Keith Clement said. The post A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4
